title: Traditional Slovakian Pork Dumplings
description: A hearty and comforting dish from the heart of Europe, featuring rich, flaky Slovakian pastry wrapped around tender pork and simmered in a savory broth.
instructions: In a large pan over medium-high heat, cook the ground pork until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pan and cook until softened.
Add the flour to the pan and stir to combine. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Gradually add the water to the pan, whisking constantly. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the salt and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the Slovakian pastry to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
Use a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of pastry.
Place a tablespoon or two of the pork mixture in the center of each pastry circle.
Brush the edges of the pastry with the beaten egg and fold the pastry over the filling to form a triangle.
Press the edges of the pastry together to seal the dumplings.
Place the dumplings in a large baking dish and brush with additional egg for a golden glaze.
Bake the dumplings in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
